rationale: A is better anchored to Kafka Connect-specific, version-pinned docs and gives concrete nearby tests plus a cautious uncertainty note. B has some plausible implementation detail, but its doc anchors are broader and less topic-specific, and several test suggestions are more generic/less verified. A is more useful for an engineer tracing validation in the herder layer with lower hallucination risk.

A: May overstate ConnectorConfig as the main validator source for connector validation flow; it is an adjacent anchor, not necessarily the herder entrypoint.
Uses repo:// URIs that may not be externally verifiable from the answer alone.
B: Doc anchors are weaker and less Connect-specific than requested; the landing page and Javadocs do not directly anchor the validation flow.
Claims validateConnectorConfig(...) and nearby REST test names without sufficient verification.
